items.title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
items.authors: Mingxian Lin, Shengju Qian, Yuqi Liu et al.
items.abstract: Vision-language model (VLM) agents are increasingly deployed in interactive game environments. Yet game benchmarks for VLM agents typically report a single first-attempt score per (agent, game) pair, focus on single-agent Solo play, and lack unified protocols for evaluating heterogeneous agent class...

items.title: An Agency-Transferring Model-Free Policy Enhancement Technique
items.authors: Anton Bolychev, Georgiy Malaniya, Sinan Ibrahim et al.
items.abstract: Training reinforcement learning (RL) policies from scratch is costly: it requires careful reward and environment design, extensive tuning, and substantial computation. Yet many control problems already have a functional but suboptimal policy available as a baseline. This paper proposes a method for ...

items.title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
items.authors: Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete, Josef Valvoda et al.
items.abstract: Language models, as multi-task learners, acquire a wide range of abilities during training. A fundamental question is how much task-specific data is needed to learn a given task. Answering this for natural language is difficult: tasks are hard to delineate and can confound one another. To rigorously...

items.title: SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation
items.authors: Matthew Ho, Brian Liu, Jixuan Chen et al.
items.abstract: Advanced scientific simulators expose specialized input languages that turn simulation goals into executable configurations, but learning them can cost domain scientists hours to days. We study simulator setup as a problem of agent-tool interface grounding: what minimal simulator-specific adaptation...

items.title: Latent Spatial Memory for Video World Models
items.authors: Weijie Wang, Haoyu Zhao, Yifan Yang et al.
items.abstract: Video world models that maintain 3D spatial consistency across generated frames typically rely on explicit point cloud memory constructed in RGB space. This design is both computationally expensive, requiring repeated rendering and VAE encoding, and inherently lossy, as the round trip through pixel ...

items.title: MemoryVLA++: Temporal Modeling via Memory and Imagination in Vision-Language-Action Models
items.authors: Hao Shi, Weiye Li, Bin Xie et al.
items.abstract: Temporal modeling is essential for robotic manipulation, as effective control requires both memory of past interactions and imagination of future states. However, most VLA models rely primarily on the current observation and therefore struggle with long-horizon, temporally dependent tasks. Cognitive...

items.title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
items.authors: Jiarui Yao, Xiangxin Zhou, Penghui Qi et al.
items.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component of post-training large language models (LLMs). In practice, LLM RL is often off-policy because of training-inference mismatch and policy staleness, making trust-region control essential for stable optimization. Mainstream methods such as PPO and...

items.title: iMaC: Translating Actions into Motion and Contact Images for Embodied World Models
items.authors: Zhenyu Wu, Xiuwei Xu, Yukun Zhou et al.
items.abstract: Embodied world models have emerged as a pivotal paradigm for visual robotic decision-making and interactive environment simulation. However, conventional embodied frameworks rely on low-dimensional structured action vectors (e.g., joint angles and end-effector poses), which suffer from limited expre...
